questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A linearly polarised electromagnetic wave in vacuum is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>E</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>1</mn><mi>cos</mi><mfenced open="[" close="]"><mrow><mfenced><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>8</mn></mrow></mfenced><mi>z</mi><mo>-</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>5</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>6</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><mi>t</mi></mrow></mfenced><mover><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mo>^</mo></mover><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> is incident normally on a perfectly reflecting wall at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>z</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>a</mi></math>. Choose the correct option.
options: The frequency of electromagnetic wave is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>54</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>4</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi>Hz</mi></math>.

The transmitted wave will be
3.1cos1.8z-5.4×106ti^ N/C.

The reflected wave will be
3.1cos1.8z+5.4×106ti^ N C-1.
questions.solution: Reflected wave will have direction opposite to incident wave.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
If E→ and K→ represent electric field and propagation vectors of the EM waves in vacuum, then magnetic field vector is given by:

(ω- angular frequency)
questions.solution:
As we know, direction of electromagnetic wave will be in the direction of the vector E→×B→.

Magnitude of B=Ec=KωE

⇒B→=1ωK→×E→
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Which of the following Maxwell’s equation is valid for time varying conditions but not valid for static conditions:
questions.solution:
Maxwell’s four equations describe the electric and magnetic fields arising from distributions of electric charges and currents, and how those fields change in time.

The equations are as follows:

∮E→·dA→=qε0   ...1∮B⇀·dA⇀=0   ...2∮E⇀·dl⇀=-∂ϕB∂t   ...3∮B⇀·dl⇀=μ0I   ...4

From the above set of equations, it is clear that only the third equation is valid for time varying field.

Hence, this is the correct option.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The electromagnetic waves travel in a medium at a speed of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mn>8</mn></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. The relative permeability of the medium is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn></math>. The relative permittivity of the medium will be
questions.solution:
Here, v=2×108 m s−1 and μr=1

The speed of electromagnetic waves in a medium is given by v=1με  ...i

where μ and ε are absolute permeability and absolute permittivity of the medium.
Now, μ=μ0μr and ε=ε0εr
Then, we have, 

v=1μ0μr×ε0εr=1μ0ε0×1μrεr=cμrεr

orεr=c2v2μr=3×10822×1082=94=2.25
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: The electric field in an electromagnetic wave is given by <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>E</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>56</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mi>sin</mi><mi>ω</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mi>t</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow><mi>c</mi></mfrac></mfenced><msup><mi>NC</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. Find the intensity of the wave if it is propagating along <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math>-axis in the free space. (Given <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>ϵ</mi><mn>0</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>8</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>85</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>12</mn></mrow></msup><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><msup><mrow><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>)
questions.solution:
Given, E=56.5sinωt-xc N C-1

Comparing it with the general equation of electromagnetic wave, 

E=E0sin ωt-kx

We have, E0=56.5 N C-1

So, the intensity will be  I=12ε0E02c

=12×8.85×10-12×56.52×3×108

=4.24  W m-2
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Given below are two statements:

Statement I : Out of microwaves, infrared rays and ultraviolet rays, ultraviolet rays are the most effective for the emission of electrons from a metallic surface

Statement II : Above the threshold frequency, the maximum kinetic energy of photoelectrons is inversely proportional to the frequency of the incident light

In the light of above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below
options: Statement I is false but Statement II is true
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
questions.solution:
Out of the three types of radiation, UV rays would be having highest frequency. The maximum kinetic energy is 

 Kmax=hf-ϕ

Clearly, the maximum kinetic energy is dependant on the frequency of the incident radiation and the work function of the metal.

From the given spectrums UV rays have the highest energy. Hence, this option is the correct one.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A screw gauge has 50 divisions on its circular scale. The circular scale is 4 units ahead of the pitch scale marking, prior to use. Upon one complete rotation of the circular scale, a displacement of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mi>mm</mi></math> is noticed on the pitch scale. The nature of zero error involved and the lest count of the screw gauge, are respectively:
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A student measuring the diameter of a pencil of circular cross-section with the help of a vernier scale records the following four readings <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>50</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>55</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>34</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>65</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math>. The average of these four reading is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5375</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math> and the standard deviation of the data is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>07395</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math>. The average diameter of the pencil should therefore be recorded as :
questions.solution:
Significant rule says that reading should has same significant figure as that of reading given.

5.5375→ rounded to →5.54.

0.07395→ rounded to→0.07

therefore, 5.54±0.07 mm
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
A screw gauge of pitch 0.5 mm is used to measure the diameter of uniform wire of length 6.8 cm, the main scale reading is 1.5 mm and circular scale reading is 7. The calculated curved surface area of wire to appropriate significant figures is
[Screw gauge has 50 divisions on the circular scale]
questions.solution:
Least Count (L.C)=PN=0.5 mm50=0.01 mm

Length of wire =6.8 cm

Diameter of wire =1.5 mm+7× L.C

=1.5 mm+7×.01=1.57 mm

Curved surface area =πDl

=3.14×6.8×1.57×10-1 cm2

=3.352 cm2=3.4 cm2
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: In a Vernier Caliper <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn></math> divisions of Vernier scale is equal to the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>9</mn></math> divisions of main scale. When both jaws of Vernier calipers touch each other, the zero of the Vernier scale is shifted to the left of zero of the main scale and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>4</mn><mi>th</mi></msup></math> Vernier scale division exactly coincides with the main scale reading. One main scale division is equal to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math>. While measuring diameter of a spherical body, the body is held between two jaws. It is now observed that zero of the Vernier scale lies between <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>30</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>31</mn></math> divisions of main scale reading and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>6</mn><mrow><mi>th</mi><mo> </mo></mrow></msup></math> Vernier scale division exactly. coincides with the main scale reading. The diameter of the spherical body will be:
questions.solution:
Given that 1 M.S.D=1 mm

And 9 M.S.D =10 V.S.D

⇒1 V.S.D=0.9M.S.D=0.9 mm

L.C of vernier caliper =1-0.9=0.1 mm=0.01 cm

Zero error =10-4×0.1 mm=0.6 mm as at zero the, the zero of the vernier is shifted towards left zero error is negative. Therefore, zero error=-0.6 mm.

Reading =M.S.R+V.S.R-  Zero error

Reading=3+6×0.01--0.06

Reading=3+0.06+0.06

Reading=3.12 cm

The closest option is 3.10 cm
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
In an experiment to find out the diameter of wire using screw gauge, the following observation were noted:

(a) Screw moves 0.5 mm on main scale in one complete rotation
(b) Total divisions on circular scale =50
(c) Main scale reading is 2.5 mm
(d) 45th  division of circular scale is in the pitch line
(e) Instrument has 0.03 mm negative error Then the diameter of wire is :
questions.solution:
From the data given in the question,

Pitch of the screw gauge=0.5 mm.

Total divisions on the circular scalen=50.

Therefore, the least count of the screw gauge is LC=pitchn=0.550=0.01 mm

Now, MSR=2.5 mm, CSR=45

Diameter reading =MSR+LC×CSR- zero error

⇒d=2.5+0.45--0.03

⇒d=2.98 mm
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: In an experiment with vernier callipers of least count <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>0</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>mm</mi></math>, when two jaws are joined together the zero of vernier scale lies right to the zero of the main scale and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>6</mn><mi>th</mi></msup></math> division of vernier scale coincides with the main scale division. While measuring the diameter of a spherical bob, the zero of vernier scale lies in between <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>3</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> marks and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>4</mn><mi>th</mi></msup></math> division of vernier scale coincides with the main scale division. The diameter of bob is measured as
questions.solution:
The least count is LC=0.1 mm

Zero error (ZE)=6×LC=6×0.1 mm=0.6 mm

The total reading is TR=MSR+VSR×LC-ZE⇒TR=32 mm+(0.1×4) mm-0.6 mm⇒TR=31.8 mm=3.18 cm
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Identify the operation performed by the circuit given below :
questions.solution:
Behaves like a NOT gate so boolean equation will be 

y=A¯+B¯+C¯¯

y=A·B·C

Whole arrangement behaves like an AND gate
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: For extrinsic semiconductors; when doping level is increased;
options: Fermi-level of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math>- type semiconductors will not be affected.

Fermi-level of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi></math>-type semiconductor will go upward and Fermi-level of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math>-type semiconductors will go downward.

Fermi-level of both p-type and n-type semiconductors will go upward for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>T</mi><mo>></mo><msub><mi>T</mi><mi>F</mi></msub><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi></math> and downward for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>T</mi><mo><</mo><msub><mi>T</mi><mi>F</mi></msub><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">K</mi><mo>,</mo></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>T</mi><mi>F</mi></msub></math> is Fermi temperature.

Fermi-level of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>p</mi></math>- type semiconductors will go downward and Fermi-level of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math>- type semiconductor will go upward.
questions.solution:
In n-type semiconductor pentavalent impurity is added. Each pentavalent impurity donates a free electron. So the Fermi-level of n -type semiconductor will go upward.
& In p-type semiconductor trivalent impurity is added. Each trivalent impurity creates a hole in the valence band. So the Fermi-level of p-type semiconductor will go downward.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Choose the correct waveform that can represent the voltage across R of the following circuit, assuming the diode is ideal one:
questions.solution:
Current will flow from the diode when diode is in forward biased.
Thus ΔV=(10sinωt-3)V and ΔV will be 'zero', when diode is reverse biased.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
In the circuit, the logical value of A=1 or B=1 when potential at A or B is 5 V and the logical value of A=0 or B=0 when potential at A or B is 0V.

The truth table of the given circuit will be :
questions.solution:
When both A and B have logical value ‘1’ both diode are reverse bias and current will flow in resistor hence output will be 5 volt i.e. logical value ‘1’.

In all other case conduction will take place, hence output will be zero volt i.e. logical value ‘0’. So truth table is

ABY000010100111

Thus, given circuit is equivalent to an AND gate.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: In the following circuit, the correct relation between output Y and inputs A and B will be
questions.solution:
When both of the inputs are given with +10 V that is logical 1, both of the diodes are in OFF condition and hence supply voltage will appear at the base terminal of the transistor which makes it switched ON and supply voltage gets a path to the ground through this transistors. Therefore, it represents NAND gate.

Hence, Y=A·B
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: Identify the solar cell characteristics from the following options :
questions.solution:
The I-V curve for solar cell is available in the fourth quadrant, i.e. where current is negative, which means that current I is supplied by the solar cell and not drawn by it.

In the given figure, the point where the graph touches the x-axis shows the open circuit voltage while the point where it touches the y-axis shows the short circuit current.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: In the given circuit the input voltage Vin  is shown in figure. The cut-in voltage of p-n junction diode (D1 or D2) is 0.6 V. Which of the following output voltage V0 waveform across the diode is correct?
questions.solution:
During the positive half cycle of the input waveform, diode D1 is forward biased and diode D2 is reverse biased. Here 0.6 V appears across the output as the diode is conducting. During negative half cycle the diode D1 is reverse biased and D2 is forward biased, so, -0.6 V appears across the output as the diode is conducting in negative direction. This circuit can be used as a voltage limiter. This circuit will not allow any voltage above 0.6 Vand below -0.6 V.

Hence, the output waveform is
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
Given below are two statements : one is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R

Assertion A: Photodiodes are used in forward bias usually for measuring the light intensity.

Reason R: For a p-n junction diode, at applied voltage V the current in the forward bias is more than the current in the reverse bias for Vz>±V≥V0 where V0 is the threshold voltage and Vz is the breakdown voltage.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below
options: Both A and R are true and R is correct explanation A
Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation A
A is false but R is true
A is true but R is false
questions.solution:
Photodiodes are operated in reverse bias condition. The change in saturation reverse current in a photodiode with reverse bias is directly proportional to the change in light intensity, thus can be precisely measured.

Current in forward bias (for applied voltageV≥V0) is always greater than current in reverse bias (for V≤Vz ) in a p-n junction diode.

Hence, Assertion if false but Reason is true.
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q:
The output Y for the inputs A and B of circuit is given by

Truth table of the shown circuit is :
questions.solution:
Output using DeMorgan's theorem can be written as,

Y=A·A·B·A·B·B=A·A·B+A·B·B=A·A·B+A·B·B=A·A+B+A+B·B=A·A+A·B+A·B+B·B=A+B·A+B

Which represents XOR gate.

Therefore, required truth table will be

ABY000011101110
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
questions.q: A zener diode of power rating 1.6 W is to be used as voltage regulator. If the zener diode has a breakdown of 8 V and it has to regulate voltage fluctuating between 3 V and 10 V The value of resistance Rs for safe operation of diode will be
questions.solution:
Power rating of zener diode =1.6 W

From the diagram above,

VA-VB=8 V

Power is given by

 P=Vi⇒i=1.6 W8 V=0.2 A

Also, VC-VA=10 V-8 V=2 V

Thus, using Ohm's law,

Rs=2 V0.2 A=10 Ω
questions.source: Top 250 Single Correct Qs of JEE Main 2023-2020
